title: tilia americana subsp. heterophylla
intro: Tilia americana subsp. heterophylla, also known as the American linden or large-toothed basswood, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its large, heart-shaped leaves and fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of Tilia americana subsp. heterophylla include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Tilia americana subsp. heterophylla is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed on its own or combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
